steps.title: Emphasize the year
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize when Rina Goldstein won the A.M. Turing Award. The notes give one timing detail: she won in 2012. The strongest sentence should put that year in the focus position instead of emphasizing the award's purpose or Goldstein's work.

steps.title: Reject non-timing focus
steps.content: A emphasizes the reason for the award, C only identifies Goldstein as a winner, and D describes the award generally. The choice that says It was in 2012 that puts the year at the front and makes the timing the main point of the sentence. The answer is B.
